questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 1:8-9 says obeying a father's instruction and a mother's teaching will be like what for your head?

answersList.value: A garland to grace your head

questionsList.questionContent: According to Proverbs 14:1 (NIV), the wise woman does what?
Proverbs 3:3 says: 'Let love and faithfulness never leave you; write them on the tablet of your ___.'
What does Proverbs 10:4 (NIV) say 'diligent hands' bring?
Proverbs 10:22 says: 'The blessing of the LORD brings wealth, without ___ toil for it.'
Proverbs 31:8 commands us to 'speak up for those who ___.'
Proverbs 4:18 says the path of the righteous is like 'the morning sun, shining ever brighter till ___.'
Proverbs 11:3 (NIV) says the integrity of the upright ___.
According to Proverbs 21:2 (NIV), a person 'may think their own ways are right' — but who 'weighs the heart'?
What does Proverbs 16:31 (NIV) call grey hair?
Proverbs 19:14 says a prudent wife comes from whom?
Proverbs 22:7 (NIV) says: 'the borrower is ___ to the lender.'
What does Proverbs 3:27 say about doing good to those who deserve it?

answersList.value: Do not withhold it when it is in your power
Build a lasting reputation by it

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 21:13 say will happen to one who 'shuts their ears to the cry of the poor'?

answersList.value: They too will cry out and not be answered
They will be rejected by the community
Their children will suffer for it

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 31:20 say the wife of noble character does for the poor and needy?

answersList.value: Teaches her household to care for them
Prays and fasts on their behalf
Opens her arms and extends her hands to them

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 10:19 say is found 'when words are many'?
Proverbs 3:13 (NIV) says: 'Blessed are those who find ___.'
What does Proverbs 24:17-18 warn against doing when your enemy falls?
What does Proverbs 20:22 say NOT to do when someone wrongs you?

answersList.value: Say 'I'll pay you back for this wrong'

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 11:28 says: 'Those who trust in their riches will ___.'
Proverbs 16:4 says the LORD works out everything to its proper end — even whom?

answersList.value: The wicked, for a day of disaster
Those who have strayed from his commands
Events that appear to be beyond his purpose

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 28:6 says: 'Better the poor whose walk is blameless than the rich whose ways are ___.'
What does Proverbs 14:26 (NIV) say the fear of the LORD provides?
What does Proverbs 12:11 say about 'those who work their land'?

answersList.value: They earn the respect of all

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 27:5 say is 'better than hidden love'?
Proverbs 22:9 (NIV) says: 'The generous will themselves be blessed, for they ___.'

answersList.value: share their food with the poor
walk in the way of righteousness

questionsList.questionContent: According to Proverbs 5:21, 'a man's ways are in full view of ___.'
Proverbs 16:1 says 'to humans belong the plans of the heart' — but from the LORD comes what?

answersList.value: The power to carry out those plans
The proper answer of the tongue

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 11:24 says one gives freely and yet gains even more — but another 'withholds unduly' and comes to what?
Proverbs 31:26 says that when the wife of noble character speaks, 'faithful instruction is on her ___.'
Proverbs 11:4 says wealth is 'worthless in the day of ___.'
According to Proverbs 15:16 (NIV), what is 'better than great wealth with turmoil'?

answersList.value: A righteous name free from reproach
Little with the fear of the LORD
The blessing of God on simple things

questionsList.questionContent: In Proverbs 23:26, what does the father ask of his son?

answersList.value: Give me your heart and keep to my ways
Walk faithfully in the path I have shown you
Never bring shame upon our name
Honour your mother all your days

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 29:2 say happens 'when the righteous thrive'?
What does Proverbs 30:6 say you should not do to 'his words'?
What does Proverbs 27:19 (NIV) compare to 'water reflecting a face'?

answersList.value: A man's deeds in God's sight
A friend who truly knows you
The mirror of a righteous life

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 2:6 says 'from his mouth come ___.'
What does Proverbs 18:16 say a gift 'opens the way for the giver' and ushers them into?

answersList.value: The favour of all they meet
Doors that were closed to them

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 19:18 say a parent should do 'while there is hope'?
According to Proverbs 21:30, no wisdom, no insight, and no plan can succeed against whom?
Proverbs 21:31 says 'the horse is made ready for the day of battle' — but what rests with the LORD?
What does Proverbs 24:10 say if 'you falter in a time of trouble'?

answersList.value: You have not trusted the LORD
You have not sought wise counsel

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 31:4-5 warns kings and rulers not to drink wine or crave beer — because they might do what?

answersList.value: Set a bad example for their people
Lose the respect of their court
Pervert justice and deprive the oppressed of their rights

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 12:14 say 'the mouth of the wise' brings them?
Proverbs 6:20-23 says a father's command is a lamp, and a mother's teaching is what?
How does Proverbs 14:27 (NIV) describe 'the fear of the LORD'?

answersList.value: A shield in the day of trouble
A fountain of life, turning a person from the snares of death
A sure foundation for all of life

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 28:14 calls 'blessed' the one who always does what?
What does Proverbs 3:11 say you should NOT do with 'the LORD's discipline'?

answersList.value: Despise it or resent his rebuke
Take it lightly or flee from it
Question it or doubt his love

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 13:7 says one person 'pretends to be rich yet has nothing.' What does the other do?

answersList.value: Flaunts wealth they do not own
Earns greatly while living as a pauper
Pretends to be poor yet has great wealth

questionsList.questionContent: According to Proverbs 31:28, who 'arise and call her blessed'?
According to Proverbs 8:15-16, what does Wisdom say kings and rulers do 'by her'?

answersList.value: Earn the loyalty of their people
Build legacies that outlast their reign
Reign and issue decrees that are just

questionsList.questionContent: In Proverbs 9:13-15, where exactly does Lady Folly sit as she calls out to passersby?

answersList.value: At the highest point of the city

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 19:1 (NIV) say is 'better' than a fool whose lips are perverse?

answersList.value: A poor man whose walk is blameless
A wise man who holds his tongue
A rich man who fears the LORD
A poor man with righteous works

questionsList.questionContent: In Proverbs 22:16, what happens to the one who 'oppresses the poor to increase his wealth'?
What do Proverbs 8:32-33 urge those who listen to Wisdom to do?

answersList.value: Seek her early and give themselves fully to her
Come to her house and sit at her table
Forsake all folly and walk in her paths
Keep to her ways and do not neglect her instruction

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 25:4-5 uses the image of removing dross from silver to illustrate what?

answersList.value: Removing the wicked so a king's throne is established in righteousness
Searching the Scriptures for hidden wisdom
Testing a friend's motives before trusting them

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 29:7 say 'the righteous' care about, in contrast to the wicked?
According to Proverbs 11:25 (NIV), the one who 'refreshes others' will themselves be what?
What does Proverbs 25:8-9 caution against hastily doing with a dispute?

answersList.value: Making a vow about it before you know the outcome
Involving others before the matter is resolved
Bringing the matter to the city gates prematurely
Taking your neighbour to court without settling it privately first

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 21:31 says the horse is made ready for battle — but what does it conclude?
Proverbs 17:8 says a bribe is like a 'charm' in the eyes of the one who gives it — wherever it turns, what happens?
What does Proverbs 13:12 (NIV) say 'hope deferred' does?
Proverbs 11:28 says those who trust in riches will fall — but the righteous will thrive like what?
What does Proverbs 23:22 urge regarding a father who 'gave you life' and a mother 'when she is old'?

answersList.value: Honour them above your own desires
Listen to him and do not despise her
Keep their counsel close to your heart
Seek their blessing before all else

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 26:12 says there is 'more hope' for a fool than for whom?

answersList.value: A person wise in their own eyes
A wicked man who will not repent

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 27:4 says jealousy surpasses anger and fury. What question closes the verse?

What does Proverbs 24:6 say 'victory' is won through?
Proverbs 27:10 says better than a brother far away is what?
What does Proverbs 12:1 (NIV) call the person who 'hates correction'?
Proverbs 22:15 says folly is 'bound up in the heart of a child' — but what drives it far away?
Proverbs 8:17 says Wisdom loves 'those who love her.' What does she promise those who seek her earnestly?
What does Proverbs 16:11 (NIV) say 'honest scales and balances' belong to?
What does Proverbs 13:22 say 'a good person' leaves behind?

answersList.value: A name that will never be forgotten
A legacy of wisdom and instruction
An inheritance for their children's children
The fruit of all their faithful years

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 30:4 asks who has gone up to heaven and come down, and ends by asking what?

answersList.value: 'What is his name, and what is the name of his son?'
'And where were you when all these things were made?'
'Have you understood this since your birth?'
'How great is your throne, and who can challenge it?'

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 23:5 say will happen to riches 'when you cast your eyes on them'?

answersList.value: They blind the heart to what truly matters
They make a man's sleep uneasy
They lead to striving that brings no peace
They are gone — they sprout wings and fly off to the sky like an eagle

questionsList.questionContent: According to Proverbs 26:14, a sluggard turning on his bed is likened to what?

answersList.value: A door turning on its hinges

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 28:19 says those who 'chase fantasies' will have their fill of what?

answersList.value: 'I'll pay them back for what they did'
'Let justice run its course for them'
'I will repay them in their own time'
'They will get exactly what they deserve'

questionsList.questionContent: According to Proverbs 30:11-14, what does the fourth 'generation' do with its teeth and jaws?

answersList.value: Tears at all who oppose them
Devours its own kin without mercy
Devours the poor from the earth and the needy from among mankind

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 25:13 compares a 'trustworthy messenger' to what?

answersList.value: The coolness of snow at harvest time

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 8:35-36 says: 'those who find me find life and receive favour from the LORD.' What happens to those who fail to find Wisdom?

answersList.value: Lose what was born to them

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 26:20 say happens to a quarrel 'without a gossip'?

Proverbs 28:1 says the wicked flee though no one pursues — but the righteous are as bold as what?
What does Proverbs 19:14 call a prudent wife, contrasting her with 'house and wealth inherited from parents'?
What does Proverbs 18:22 (NIV) say about finding a wife?

answersList.value: He who finds a wife finds what is good and receives favour from the LORD
It requires careful prayer and seeking
It is a lifelong covenant before God
She reflects a man's own character

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 22:15 says folly is 'bound up in the heart of a child.' What does the rod of discipline do?
Proverbs 17:9 says 'whoever would foster love covers over an offence.' But what does one who 'repeats the matter' do?

Entrust the matter to a righteous judge
Wait for the LORD, and he will avenge you
Pray for the one who has wronged you

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 16:3 says: 'Commit to the LORD whatever you do, and ___.'
Proverbs 25:17 warns: 'Seldom set foot in your neighbour's house — lest they have ___.'

answersList.value: No choice but to reject you
Cause to take advantage of your kindness
Too much of you and hate you

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 10:25 says 'when the storm has swept by, the wicked are gone.' But what does it say about the righteous?

answersList.value: The righteous stand firm for ever
Kept in peace by the LORD

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 17:15 says the LORD 'detests' two things — acquitting the guilty and what?

answersList.value: Bearing false witness against a neighbour

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 24:3-4 says a house is built by wisdom and established by understanding. Through what are 'its rooms filled with rare and beautiful treasures'?
What does Proverbs 11:13 say 'a gossip' does with secrets?
Proverbs 14:31 says the one who 'oppresses the poor' shows contempt for whom?
Proverbs 18:17 says 'in a lawsuit the first to speak seems right — until ___.'

answersList.value: The judge weighs all the evidence
The truth is brought to light
Someone comes forward and cross-examines them
The other party has their say

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 13:4 says the desires of the diligent are ___.
What does Proverbs 15:22 say about plans that lack counsel?
According to Proverbs 8:23 (NIV), Wisdom was 'appointed from eternity, from the beginning, ___.'

Proverbs 30:6 warns 'do not add to his words' or he will rebuke you and prove you ___.
In Proverbs 7:8-9, what time of day does the naive young man take the road toward the adulteress's house?

answersList.value: At midnight, heading toward the city
At sunset, going toward the temple mount
At twilight as the evening approached and the dark of night set in
Early in the morning, wandering aimlessly

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 6:4-5 urges the one who has pledged security for a neighbour to free himself like what two animals?

answersList.value: A lion from the net and a bird from the snare
A gazelle from the hand of the hunter and a bird from the fowler's snare
A deer from the arrow and a dove from the cage
A wild horse from the rein and an eagle from the cage

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 26:18-19 describes someone who deceives a neighbour and says 'I was only joking.' What image does it use?
In Proverbs 7:20, the adulteress says her husband took his purse and will not be home until what?
Proverbs 25:20 uses two images for singing songs to a heavy heart. What are they?

answersList.value: Pouring oil on water and lighting a candle in a blizzard
Adding vinegar to milk and putting a stone over a spring
Taking away a garment on a cold day and pouring vinegar on a wound
Cutting thorns bare-handed and running into a burning building

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 5:22 says 'the evil deeds of the wicked ensnare them.' What holds them fast?
What does Proverbs 27:21 say about 'the crucible for silver and the furnace for gold'?

answersList.value: Both refine, but men are refined only through suffering
Only what endures the fire has lasting worth
All true treasure comes through fire and toil
But people are tested by the praise they receive

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 28:9 says that even 'the prayers' of those who turn a deaf ear to the law are what?
Proverbs 6:32 says a man who commits adultery 'has no sense.' What does he do to himself?
What literary structure does Proverbs 31:10-31 follow in the original Hebrew?

answersList.value: A repeated refrain built around the name of God
An acrostic poem following the letters of the Hebrew alphabet
A chiastic structure with the fear of the LORD at its centre
A catalogue of virtues ordered by their importance

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 25:26 (NIV) compares what to 'a muddied spring or a polluted well'?

answersList.value: A king who turns his ear to liars
A nation with no godly leaders
A city with no walls or watchmen
A righteous person who gives way to the wicked

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 5:4 says the end of the adulteress is 'bitter as gall' and sharp as what?
Proverbs 30:11-14 describes four 'generations.' What does the fourth do with its teeth?

answersList.value: Tears at the weak who cannot defend themselves
Devours the poor from the earth and the needy from among mankind

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 26:4-5 deliberately demonstrate by placing two contradictory proverbs side by side?

answersList.value: The need for wisdom in knowing when to apply each principle
Irony to expose the foolishness of human counsel
A test of discernment embedded in the text

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 20:14 describes a buyer who says 'It's no good!' about an item — what does he do after leaving?

What does Proverbs 16:2 say the LORD 'weighs,' even though 'all a person's ways seem pure to them'?

answersList.value: A blind man at a cliff
A deer stepping into a noose

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 5:23 says the wicked will die 'for lack of discipline' — and 'led astray by ___.'

Proverbs 25:15 uses patience to persuade a ruler — and what does a 'gentle tongue' do to a bone?

answersList.value: it earns them rest the idle never know
it keeps them from the path of idleness
it is a gift from God

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 20:10 says 'differing weights and differing measures' are both what?
Proverbs 12:18 contrasts 'words that wound like a sword' with what?

answersList.value: A well-timed rebuke from a trusted friend
The tongue of the wise that brings healing
A word withheld until the right moment

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 17:12 say is 'better to encounter' than a fool bent on folly?

answersList.value: An angry king in full wrath
A wise man giving bad advice
A bear robbed of her cubs

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 6:16-19 lists seven things the LORD 'hates' and 'detests.' What is the seventh and final item?

answersList.value: A person who stirs up conflict in the community
The one who sheds innocent blood
A heart that devises wicked schemes
A lying tongue that deceives the court

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 30:33 uses churning cream and twisting a nose to illustrate what outcome?

answersList.value: Pressing a friend brings hidden secrets to light
Testing a person reveals their true character

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 15:22 says plans fail for lack of counsel — but 'with many advisers' what happens?
Proverbs 14:1 says the wise woman builds her house. What does 'the foolish one' do?

answersList.value: Tears it down with her own hands

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 13:9 says 'the light of the righteous shines brightly' — but what happens to 'the lamp of the wicked'?
Proverbs 28:4 says those who 'forsake instruction' do what, in contrast to those who 'heed it'?
Proverbs 22:26-27 warns against 'putting up security for debts.' What may be taken if you cannot pay?

answersList.value: Your very bed from under you
All the work of your hands

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 30:5 says 'every word of God is flawless.' What is he to those who 'take refuge in him'?
Proverbs 8:13 says the fear of the LORD is 'to hate evil.' What four things does Wisdom say she hates?

answersList.value: Pride, arrogance, evil behaviour, and perverse speech
Lying lips, a shedding hand, proud eyes, and an evil heart

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 29:1 say happens to the one who 'remains stiff-necked after many rebukes'?

answersList.value: They will be abandoned even by those who love them
Wisdom will no longer answer when they call
They will suddenly be destroyed — without remedy
The LORD will hand them over to their own foolishness

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 19:3 says 'a person's own folly leads to their ruin.' What does their heart then do?

answersList.value: 'You have blessed this house beyond all measure'
'Your wisdom surpasses all who have gone before you'
'No jewel of the earth could match your worth'
'Many women do noble things, but you surpass them all'

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 10:9 say about 'whoever walks in integrity'?

answersList.value: Their path is known to God

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 26:9 says 'a proverb in the mouth of a fool' is like what?

answersList.value: A sword given to a child without training
A torch carried carelessly through dry fields
A thornbush in a drunkard's hand
A broken hammer without a handle

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 24:12 asks: 'Does not he who weighs the heart perceive it?' — when you say you 'did not know' about what?

answersList.value: Those being led away to death

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 25:4 uses removing dross from silver — and verse 5 applies it to removing the wicked from whom?
Proverbs 24:13-14 compares wisdom to honey — what does it say wisdom offers for the future?

answersList.value: A hope that will not be cut off
Sweetness that no earthly pleasure can match
Life and favour with God and man

questionsList.questionContent: What does Proverbs 16:7 say happens when 'the LORD takes pleasure in anyone's way'?

answersList.value: Their prayers are answered before they rise
He grants them favour before all people
He causes their enemies to make peace with them
Their enemies are turned into friends by grace

questionsList.questionContent: According to Proverbs 18:19, what is 'more unyielding than a fortified city'?

answersList.value: A brother who has been wronged

questionsList.questionContent: Proverbs 25:2 says it is 'the glory of God to conceal a matter.' What is 'the glory of kings'?
